mitigations.label: fee-redirect-only
mitigations.description: Setting feeTo only redirects PancakeSwap V2's protocol-fee share (~1/8 of LP swap fees, minted via _mintFee on each PancakePair mint/burn) to a different address. LP token reserves in pair contracts cannot be moved by the factory. Existing liquidity providers retain their full deposits and the remaining 7/8 of swap fees they would normally accrue. Pairs continue functioning normally. The multisig (or any new owner) cannot withdraw, pause, or otherwise touch the $1.77B in LP positions.

mitigations.label: fee-redirect-only
mitigations.description: Transfers the feeToSetter role to a different address. The only downstream authority this grants is setFeeTo (which only redirects the protocol-fee share — see setFeeTo) and rotating the role again. PancakeSwap V2 pairs are immutable and the factory has no other admin functions, so no path exists from feeToSetter to LP deposits. The $1.77B in LP positions cannot be withdrawn, paused, or upgraded by any holder of this role.

mitigations.label: owner-mint
mitigations.description: MasterChef V1 mints arbitrary CAKE supply via this function (called from updatePool every block to issue 40 CAKE: 10% to devaddr, 90% to syrup pool / MasterChef V2). The mint amount per call is controlled by MasterChef V1 logic (cakePerBlock × elapsed blocks × multiplier), not capped at the CakeToken level.

mitigations.label: owner-mint
mitigations.description: Variant of mint that takes a recipient address. Currently only invoked by MasterChef V1; would mint to an arbitrary address if its owner (MasterChef V1) were replaced via CakeToken.transferOwnership.

mitigations.label: authority-transfer
mitigations.description: Transfers CakeToken ownership (= mint authority) to an arbitrary address. Currently held by MasterChef V1 (which is itself owned by the MigrationHelper, treated as frozen by DeFiScan). A successful call would grant the new owner uncapped CAKE-mint power.

mitigations.label: authority-renounce
mitigations.description: Renounces CakeToken ownership. Permanently disables CAKE minting (since only owner can mint). Considered critical because it would brick MasterChef V1’s 40-CAKE/block emissions stream.

mitigations.label: frozen-owner
mitigations.description: Owner = MigrationHelper, reported by DeFiScan as functionally uncallable. Even if executable, only affects FUTURE CAKE distribution rate per pool (BONUS_MULTIPLIER), not extracting committed funds.

mitigations.label: frozen-owner
mitigations.description: Adds a new pool to MasterChef V1. Owner = MigrationHelper (frozen). Affects future CAKE allocation only; cannot move existing user deposits.

mitigations.label: frozen-owner
mitigations.description: Updates allocation points for an existing MasterChef V1 pool. Owner = MigrationHelper (frozen). Future-only effect on CAKE distribution.

mitigations.label: frozen-owner-migrator-attack-vector
mitigations.description: Sets the migrator address for V1’s migrate() function. Owner = MigrationHelper (frozen). If owner control is recovered, this is the classic Sushi-style migrator attack vector: a malicious migrator could rug all LP tokens staked in V1 pools. Treated as no-impact under the assumption the owner is genuinely frozen — a verified attestation that MigrationHelper has no callable owner-rotation path would harden this.

mitigations.label: authority-transfer-frozen
mitigations.description: Owner is MigrationHelper, which DeFiScan reports as functionally uncallable (no callable owner-changing path exposed). If that analysis holds, this entry is critical-but-frozen — if it does NOT hold, a fresh owner inherits all of MasterChef V1’s powers including CAKE mint authority via CakeToken.owner = MasterChef V1.

mitigations.label: authority-renounce-frozen
mitigations.description: Same frozen-owner caveat as transferOwnership.

mitigations.label: dev-rotation
mitigations.description: Rotates the recipient of 10% of MasterChef V1's 40 CAKE/block emissions. Cap = ~1 week of diverted emissions before a 3-of-7 multisig revert.

mitigations.label: one-shot-initialized
mitigations.description: Initializer for MasterChef V2 (sets the dummy LP token used in MasterChef V1 pool 0). Already called at deployment; effectively dead path.

mitigations.label: future-rewards-only
mitigations.description: Adds a new farm with allocation points. Affects FUTURE CAKE distribution split; cannot move existing staked LP tokens. No migrator on V2.

mitigations.label: future-rewards-only
mitigations.description: Reweights an existing farm’s CAKE share. Future-only effect; existing deposits and accrued-but-unclaimed rewards are preserved.

mitigations.label: anti-extraction
mitigations.description: Mints the burn-share of MasterChef V1 emissions and immediately burns them. Anti-extraction primitive; no path to user funds.

mitigations.label: future-rewards-only
mitigations.description: Changes the burn / regular-farm / special-farm split of MasterChef V1’s 40 CAKE/block. Subject to per-rate caps in source. Future-only effect on reward distribution.

mitigations.label: whitelist-future-only
mitigations.description: Toggles boost-eligibility for a single address. Affects which addresses can have boostMultiplier > 1; no fund-extraction path.

mitigations.label: boost-rewire
mitigations.description: Replaces the contract that can call updateBoostMultiplier. A malicious boost contract could zero existing users’ boost multipliers (capping subsequent CAKE rewards at the unboosted rate) and grant attacker-controlled addresses 2-2.5× multipliers. Affects future reward accrual only — cannot extract staked LP tokens.

mitigations.label: authority-transfer
mitigations.description: Transfers MasterChef V2 ownership away from the 6h Timelock. A non-timelock owner would remove the only on-chain delay-window protection on MasterChef V2 changes (allocation points, cake rate, boost contract, etc.).

mitigations.label: authority-renounce
mitigations.description: Renounces MasterChef V2 ownership; permanently disables future farm-allocation / cake-rate changes. Existing user deposits remain withdrawable (no migrator on V2).

mitigations.label: burn-admin-rewire
mitigations.description: Rotates the burnAdmin role. The new burn admin can call burnCake() which mints CAKE from MasterChef V1 to MasterChef V2 and immediately burns it; no extraction path. Critical because the role gates a function with on-chain mint side-effects, but the side-effects are anti-extraction.

mitigations.label: per-user-boost-write
mitigations.description: Sets userInfo[pid][user].boostMultiplier for any user. Restricted to the current boostContract (FarmBooster). Affects subsequent CAKE-reward accrual for the targeted user; does not touch staked LP tokens or already-claimed rewards. Per-user impact only.

mitigations.label: admin-queues-arbitrary-call
mitigations.description: GnosisSafeProxy #2 (admin) can queue any call to any target via this Timelock. Subject to the Timelock’s current 6.03h delay (delay = 21700s; MINIMUM_DELAY = 21600s). Currently used to execute MasterChef V2 onlyOwner calls.

mitigations.label: admin-cancels-queued
mitigations.description: Admin can cancel any queued transaction prior to execution. No fund-impact path of its own; included for completeness.

mitigations.label: admin-executes-queued
mitigations.description: After the 6h delay, admin executes a previously queued transaction. The fund-impact bound depends on the target/call — typically MasterChef V2 onlyOwner functions, all of which are themselves scored individually.

mitigations.label: self-can-shorten-delay
mitigations.description: Only callable by the Timelock itself (i.e. via queueTransaction). Bounded by MINIMUM_DELAY = 21600s (6h) and MAXIMUM_DELAY = 2592000s (30 days). The 6h floor is the protocol’s only hard guarantee against zero-delay rugs.

mitigations.label: self-rotates-admin
mitigations.description: Only callable by the Timelock itself. Sets a new pendingAdmin who must then call acceptAdmin. Effectively rotates control of the entire MasterChef V2 governance pipeline.

mitigations.label: dead-path-zero-pending
mitigations.description: Only callable by pendingAdmin, which is currently bnb:0x0000…0000 (zero address). Effectively dead until setPendingAdmin queues a non-zero pending admin.
